GHOST MCP Interface Requirements
Traceability
  • VIEWS
  • Document
  • Table
  • Traceability
  • Deep Traceability

    • 1. 1. Purpose and Scope
    This document defines the remote Model Context Protocol interface through which an approved AI client submits one prompt to GHOST and receives the corresponding engineered prompt.

    The approved processing path is limited to deterministic PreProcessing followed by A2 PromptShaper. The MCP interface is an adapter around existing GHOST prompt-processing behavior; it does not redefine that behavior and does not generate the final answer to the user's original request.
    • GHOST-UI-ROOT
      1.1. GHOST Interface Family Scope UID: GHOST-UI-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-UI-INTERFACE-FAMILY Human and External Interface Family
      • GHOST-UI-NO-DUPLICATION No Duplication Across Interface Files
      • GHOST-UI-SUBSYSTEM-BOUNDARY Interface and Subsystem Responsibility Boundary
      • GHOST-UI-DEPLOYMENT-BOUNDARY Interface and Deployment Boundary
      • GHOST-UI-CHILD-STATUS Independent Child-File Lifecycle Status
      • GHOST-UI-ST-ROOT GHOST Current Streamlit Workbench Scope
      • GHOST-UI-REACT-ROOT GHOST Future React and FastAPI Interface Scope
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall organize its human workbenches and external client interfaces under one interface requirement family.

      This parent file shall define only shared scope, child-file ownership, separation rules, and status-governance rules.

      It shall not define detailed screens, controls, API schemas, MCP tools, transport settings, authentication infrastructure, deployment topology, or internal subsystem algorithms.
    GHOST-MCP-ROOT
    1.1. GHOST MCP Interface Scope UID: GHOST-MCP-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Interface Family Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
    • GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY Product and Python Package Naming Boundary
    • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
    • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR Malformed MCP Request Error
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
    • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
    • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
    STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one remote MCP interface that allows ChatGPT and Claude to submit a user prompt, execute the approved GHOST prompt-engineering path, and receive the resulting engineered prompt in the same client conversation.

    This requirement defines a target interface. The MCP capability shall not be represented as implemented until the server code and the real-client acceptance tests defined by this document have passed.
    • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW
      1.2. Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow UID: GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call
      • GHOST-MCP-EXISTING-PROMPT-SCHEMA Existing Prompt Schema Reuse
      STATEMENT: The MCP prompt-engineering flow shall be exactly:

      1. receive one user-supplied prompt;
      2. create one fresh SuperPrompt for that request;
      3. execute the existing deterministic PreProcessing function on that SuperPrompt;
      4. execute the existing A2 PromptShaper on the same SuperPrompt;
      5. obtain the engineered prompt from the final SuperPrompt prompt-ready projection; and
      6. return that engineered prompt to the invoking MCP client.

      No additional GHOST pipeline stage shall be inserted into this flow by this interface.
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP
      1.3. MCP Adapter Ownership UID: GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE PreProcessing and A2 Functional Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Agent Stack Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Prompt Projection Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE AWS Deployment Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY Native Client and GHOST UI Boundary
      STATEMENT: This document shall own the external MCP protocol contract, the exposed tool contract, request isolation, result delivery, interface-visible failures, and real-client interoperability criteria.

      It shall not own the internal functional semantics of PreProcessing or A2 PromptShaper and shall not duplicate their requirements.
    • GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY
      1.4. Product and Python Package Naming Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      STATEMENT: The product exposed through the MCP interface shall be named GHOST.

      The existing Python package namespace may remain ragstream in implementation modules and import paths. The MCP interface shall not present RAGstream as the product identity.
    • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY
      2.1.1. ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility UID: GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      • GHOST-MCP-EXPLICIT-CLIENT-INVOCATION Explicit Client Invocation Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CROSS-CLIENT-CONSISTENCY Cross-Client Interface Consistency
      STATEMENT: The same GHOST MCP server implementation shall be interoperable with:

      - the owner's ChatGPT web client through its custom MCP plugin capability; and
      - Claude through its remote custom connector capability.

      Compatibility shall be demonstrated against both real clients and shall not be inferred only from an MCP Inspector or a unit-test client.
    • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR
      4.1.1. Malformed MCP Request Error UID: GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ERROR-SANITIZATION Error Information Sanitization
      STATEMENT: A malformed JSON-RPC request, an unsupported MCP method, an unknown tool name, or a request that fails the structural MCP CallToolRequest contract shall be reported through the MCP protocol error mechanism and shall not invoke GHOST prompt-processing logic.

      Tool-specific argument validation failures shall not be classified as malformed MCP requests solely because the supplied arguments do not satisfy the ghost_engineer_prompt inputSchema.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
      4.2.1. MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE Exact Tool Inventory Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE PreProcessing-before-A2 Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE Concurrent Isolation Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE ChatGPT End-to-End Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE Claude End-to-End Acceptance
      STATEMENT: The server shall pass an acceptance sequence that performs, in order:

      1. initialize;
      2. notifications/initialized;
      3. tools/list;
      4. tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt with a valid prompt_text; and
      5. verification of the returned TextContent, structuredContent, isError state, and a2 stage.
    • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS
      5.2.1. Excluded GHOST Capabilities UID: GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-PREVIOUS-RESPONSE-CAPTURE No Previous Assistant Response Capture
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-SHARED-BLACKBOARD No Shared Blackboard in Current Contract
      STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt tool shall not execute:

      - ActiveBrief discovery, relation classification, snapshotting, or routing;
      - document ingestion;
      - dense, sparse, hybrid, or reranked document retrieval;
      - evidence selection or condensation;
      - memory recording, ingestion, retrieval, merging, or synthesis;
      - blackboard read or write operations;
      - A3, A4, or later pipeline agents;
      - file management;
      - Streamlit or React/FastAPI UI actions; or
      - final answer generation.
    • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL
      5.3.1. Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension UID: GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-CONTRACT-CHANGE-CONTROL MCP Tool Contract Change Control
      STATEMENT: A future MCP capability shall be added only through explicit requirements that define its inputs, outputs, persistence effects, authorization needs, ownership boundary, and client acceptance criteria.

      The existence of an extension idea shall not authorize silent expansion of ghost_engineer_prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT
      1.1. GHOST MCP Interface Scope UID: GHOST-MCP-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Interface Family Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY Product and Python Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR Malformed MCP Request Error
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one remote MCP interface that allows ChatGPT and Claude to submit a user prompt, execute the approved GHOST prompt-engineering path, and receive the resulting engineered prompt in the same client conversation.

      This requirement defines a target interface. The MCP capability shall not be represented as implemented until the server code and the real-client acceptance tests defined by this document have passed.
    GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW
    1.2. Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow UID: GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call
    • GHOST-MCP-EXISTING-PROMPT-SCHEMA Existing Prompt Schema Reuse
    STATEMENT: The MCP prompt-engineering flow shall be exactly:

    1. receive one user-supplied prompt;
    2. create one fresh SuperPrompt for that request;
    3. execute the existing deterministic PreProcessing function on that SuperPrompt;
    4. execute the existing A2 PromptShaper on the same SuperPrompt;
    5. obtain the engineered prompt from the final SuperPrompt prompt-ready projection; and
    6. return that engineered prompt to the invoking MCP client.

    No additional GHOST pipeline stage shall be inserted into this flow by this interface.
    • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT
      3.3.1. Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call UID: GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL Existing PreProcessing Invocation
      • GHOST-MCP-REQUEST-ISOLATION Concurrent Request Isolation
      STATEMENT: The MCP adapter shall create one new SuperPrompt for each accepted ghost_engineer_prompt call.

      It shall not reuse a mutable SuperPrompt from another request, another client, another user, or an earlier tool call.
    • GHOST-MCP-EXISTING-PROMPT-SCHEMA
      3.3.2. Existing Prompt Schema Reuse UID: GHOST-MCP-EXISTING-PROMPT-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      STATEMENT: The MCP adapter shall use the same active PromptSchema configuration used by the GHOST application for deterministic PreProcessing.

      The adapter shall not define a second MCP-specific canonical-header vocabulary, mandatory-field set, alias map, bilingual map, or default map.
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT
      1.1. GHOST MCP Interface Scope UID: GHOST-MCP-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Interface Family Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY Product and Python Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR Malformed MCP Request Error
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one remote MCP interface that allows ChatGPT and Claude to submit a user prompt, execute the approved GHOST prompt-engineering path, and receive the resulting engineered prompt in the same client conversation.

      This requirement defines a target interface. The MCP capability shall not be represented as implemented until the server code and the real-client acceptance tests defined by this document have passed.
    GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP
    1.3. MCP Adapter Ownership UID: GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE PreProcessing and A2 Functional Ownership
    • GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Agent Stack Ownership Boundary
    • GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Prompt Projection Ownership Boundary
    • GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE AWS Deployment Ownership Boundary
    • GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY Native Client and GHOST UI Boundary
    STATEMENT: This document shall own the external MCP protocol contract, the exposed tool contract, request isolation, result delivery, interface-visible failures, and real-client interoperability criteria.

    It shall not own the internal functional semantics of PreProcessing or A2 PromptShaper and shall not duplicate their requirements.
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE
      5.1.1. PreProcessing and A2 Functional Ownership UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      STATEMENT: 30_preprocessing_prompt_functions.sdoc shall remain the owner of deterministic PreProcessing behavior, SuperPrompt preparation behavior, A2 PromptShaper functional behavior, configured meta-field selection, selector validation, and prompt-ready projection outcomes used by this MCP tool.

      This document shall require reuse of those capabilities but shall not restate their internal algorithms.
    • GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE
      5.1.2. Agent Stack Ownership Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      STATEMENT: 20_agent_stack.sdoc shall remain the owner of generic AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, LLMClient, configuration loading, model-call, parsing, and neutral agent-stack infrastructure used by A2.

      The MCP adapter shall consume that infrastructure only through the existing A2 boundary.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE
      5.1.3. Prompt Projection Ownership Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      STATEMENT: The central SuperPrompt projection and prompt-ready composition requirements shall remain owned by the applicable context-integration and SuperPrompt-projection requirement files.

      The MCP adapter shall return the resulting prompt_ready value and shall not become a second prompt renderer.
    • GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE
      5.1.4. AWS Deployment Ownership Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      STATEMENT: 80_runtime_platform_and_operations.sdoc and 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc shall own the MCP container deployment, public endpoint, HTTPS termination, reverse proxy, internal port, AWS identity and network controls, secrets delivery, startup, restart, and operational verification.

      This document shall not duplicate concrete AWS account values, ports, paths, container names, ECR repositories, startup scripts, systemd units, nginx blocks, or authorization-provider implementation details.
    • GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY
      5.1.5. Native Client and GHOST UI Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      STATEMENT: The MCP server shall not own the native ChatGPT or Claude conversation UI and shall not modify the current Streamlit workbench or the future React/FastAPI interface architecture.

      This document requires the engineered prompt to be delivered to the invoking client; rendering and presentation inside the native client remain client behavior verified through acceptance testing.
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT
      1.1. GHOST MCP Interface Scope UID: GHOST-MCP-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Interface Family Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow

      • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one remote MCP interface that allows ChatGPT and Claude to submit a user prompt, execute the approved GHOST prompt-engineering path, and receive the resulting engineered prompt in the same client conversation.

      This requirement defines a target interface. The MCP capability shall not be represented as implemented until the server code and the real-client acceptance tests defined by this document have passed.
    GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY
    1.4. Product and Python Package Naming Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
    STATEMENT: The product exposed through the MCP interface shall be named GHOST.

    The existing Python package namespace may remain ragstream in implementation modules and import paths. The MCP interface shall not present RAGstream as the product identity.

    2. 2. MCP Communication Contract
    The MCP server is a remote tool provider. ChatGPT or Claude invokes the GHOST tool through MCP; the server does not passively read or intercept ordinary client conversations.

    2.1. 2.1. Client and Transport Contract
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT
      1.1. GHOST MCP Interface Scope UID: GHOST-MCP-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Interface Family Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY Product and Python Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR Malformed MCP Request Error
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one remote MCP interface that allows ChatGPT and Claude to submit a user prompt, execute the approved GHOST prompt-engineering path, and receive the resulting engineered prompt in the same client conversation.

      This requirement defines a target interface. The MCP capability shall not be represented as implemented until the server code and the real-client acceptance tests defined by this document have passed.
    GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY
    2.1.1. ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility UID: GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
    • GHOST-MCP-EXPLICIT-CLIENT-INVOCATION Explicit Client Invocation Boundary
    • GHOST-MCP-CROSS-CLIENT-CONSISTENCY Cross-Client Interface Consistency
    STATEMENT: The same GHOST MCP server implementation shall be interoperable with:

    - the owner's ChatGPT web client through its custom MCP plugin capability; and
    - Claude through its remote custom connector capability.

    Compatibility shall be demonstrated against both real clients and shall not be inferred only from an MCP Inspector or a unit-test client.
    • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP
      2.1.2. Remote Streamable HTTP Transport UID: GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS External HTTPS Requirement
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
      • GHOST-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION Streamable HTTP Origin Validation
      STATEMENT: The GHOST MCP server shall expose the MCP protocol through Streamable HTTP.

      The server shall provide one logical MCP endpoint that supports the HTTP methods required by Streamable HTTP and shall exchange valid MCP JSON-RPC messages through that endpoint.

      The concrete public hostname, path, reverse-proxy configuration, container port, and AWS network realization shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MCP-EXPLICIT-CLIENT-INVOCATION
      2.1.7. Explicit Client Invocation Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-EXPLICIT-CLIENT-INVOCATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      STATEMENT: The MCP server shall operate when the user enables or explicitly invokes the GHOST plugin or connector in ChatGPT or Claude and the client issues the MCP tool call.

      The interface shall not claim that GHOST automatically receives every ordinary message written in a native ChatGPT or Claude conversation when no GHOST tool call occurs.
    • GHOST-MCP-CROSS-CLIENT-CONSISTENCY
      4.3.3. Cross-Client Interface Consistency UID: GHOST-MCP-CROSS-CLIENT-CONSISTENCY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      STATEMENT: ChatGPT and Claude shall use the same MCP endpoint contract, tool identifier, input schema, processing sequence, and output schema.

      Client-specific UI configuration or OAuth registration details shall not create two different GHOST prompt-engineering behaviors.
    • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY
      2.1.1. ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility UID: GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      • GHOST-MCP-EXPLICIT-CLIENT-INVOCATION Explicit Client Invocation Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CROSS-CLIENT-CONSISTENCY Cross-Client Interface Consistency
      STATEMENT: The same GHOST MCP server implementation shall be interoperable with:

      - the owner's ChatGPT web client through its custom MCP plugin capability; and
      - Claude through its remote custom connector capability.

      Compatibility shall be demonstrated against both real clients and shall not be inferred only from an MCP Inspector or a unit-test client.
    GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP
    2.1.2. Remote Streamable HTTP Transport UID: GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS External HTTPS Requirement
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
    • GHOST-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION Streamable HTTP Origin Validation
    STATEMENT: The GHOST MCP server shall expose the MCP protocol through Streamable HTTP.

    The server shall provide one logical MCP endpoint that supports the HTTP methods required by Streamable HTTP and shall exchange valid MCP JSON-RPC messages through that endpoint.

    The concrete public hostname, path, reverse-proxy configuration, container port, and AWS network realization shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS
      2.1.3. External HTTPS Requirement UID: GHOST-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      STATEMENT: ChatGPT and Claude shall communicate with the remote GHOST MCP endpoint only through HTTPS.

      The MCP interface shall not require either client to connect directly to an unencrypted public HTTP endpoint or directly to an internal container port.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION
      2.1.4. MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      STATEMENT: The server shall complete the MCP initialize exchange, accept the initialized notification, negotiate a protocol version supported by both parties, and advertise the tools capability.

      The server shall not advertise resources, prompts, sampling, elicitation, tasks, or other capabilities that its implementation does not provide.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS
      2.2.1. Protected Remote Access UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA OAuth Protected Resource Metadata
      • GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT Bearer Access Token Transport
      • GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION Access Token Resource Validation
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE Authentication Failure Response
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE Authorization Failure Response
      STATEMENT: The deployed remote MCP interface shall use the MCP HTTP authorization model based on OAuth 2.1 so that ChatGPT and Claude can obtain and present access tokens through their supported remote-MCP connection flows.

      The GHOST MCP server shall act as the protected resource server. Unauthenticated or invalidly authenticated requests shall not invoke PreProcessing, A2 PromptShaper, or any other GHOST application function.

      The authorization-server product, credential storage, client registration, discovery-route deployment, token-validation infrastructure, and environment values shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION
      2.2.7. Streamable HTTP Origin Validation UID: GHOST-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      STATEMENT: For each Streamable HTTP connection, the server shall validate the Origin header when the header is present.

      A present Origin value that is not accepted by the deployed configuration shall receive HTTP 403 before the request reaches GHOST prompt-processing logic.

      The exact accepted Origin values shall be established from verified ChatGPT and Claude client behavior and recorded in the deployment profile rather than invented in this interface document.
    • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP
      2.1.2. Remote Streamable HTTP Transport UID: GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS External HTTPS Requirement
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
      • GHOST-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION Streamable HTTP Origin Validation
      STATEMENT: The GHOST MCP server shall expose the MCP protocol through Streamable HTTP.

      The server shall provide one logical MCP endpoint that supports the HTTP methods required by Streamable HTTP and shall exchange valid MCP JSON-RPC messages through that endpoint.

      The concrete public hostname, path, reverse-proxy configuration, container port, and AWS network realization shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    GHOST-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS
    2.1.3. External HTTPS Requirement UID: GHOST-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
    STATEMENT: ChatGPT and Claude shall communicate with the remote GHOST MCP endpoint only through HTTPS.

    The MCP interface shall not require either client to connect directly to an unencrypted public HTTP endpoint or directly to an internal container port.
    • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP
      2.1.2. Remote Streamable HTTP Transport UID: GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS External HTTPS Requirement
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
      • GHOST-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION Streamable HTTP Origin Validation
      STATEMENT: The GHOST MCP server shall expose the MCP protocol through Streamable HTTP.

      The server shall provide one logical MCP endpoint that supports the HTTP methods required by Streamable HTTP and shall exchange valid MCP JSON-RPC messages through that endpoint.

      The concrete public hostname, path, reverse-proxy configuration, container port, and AWS network realization shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION
    2.1.4. MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
    STATEMENT: The server shall complete the MCP initialize exchange, accept the initialized notification, negotiate a protocol version supported by both parties, and advertise the tools capability.

    The server shall not advertise resources, prompts, sampling, elicitation, tasks, or other capabilities that its implementation does not provide.
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST
      2.1.5. Tool Discovery UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL Tool Invocation
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      STATEMENT: In response to tools/list, the server shall return exactly the tool defined by this document for the approved GHOST prompt-engineering scope.

      The server shall not expose retrieval, memory, ingestion, blackboard, file-management, UI-control, answer-generation, or full-pipeline tools under this requirement set.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION
      2.1.4. MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      STATEMENT: The server shall complete the MCP initialize exchange, accept the initialized notification, negotiate a protocol version supported by both parties, and advertise the tools capability.

      The server shall not advertise resources, prompts, sampling, elicitation, tasks, or other capabilities that its implementation does not provide.
    GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST
    2.1.5. Tool Discovery UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL Tool Invocation
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
    STATEMENT: In response to tools/list, the server shall return exactly the tool defined by this document for the approved GHOST prompt-engineering scope.

    The server shall not expose retrieval, memory, ingestion, blackboard, file-management, UI-control, answer-generation, or full-pipeline tools under this requirement set.
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL
      2.1.6. Tool Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-STATELESS-REQUEST-CONTRACT Stateless Prompt Request Contract
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-RATE-LIMITING Configurable MCP Tool Rate Limiting
      STATEMENT: The server shall accept tools/call for the declared GHOST prompt-engineering tool and shall execute the tool only after the request has passed protocol, authorization, and input validation.

      A tools/call request for an undeclared tool shall not execute GHOST application logic.
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL
      3.1.1. GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE Prompt-Engineering Tool Display Title
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION Prompt-Engineering Tool Description
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS Prompt-Engineering Tool Behavioral Annotations
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT No Project or Memory Persistence Side Effect
      STATEMENT: The MCP server shall expose one tool with the stable identifier:

      ghost_engineer_prompt

      The tool shall transform one supplied prompt by executing existing GHOST PreProcessing followed by existing A2 PromptShaper and shall return the resulting engineered prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST
      2.1.5. Tool Discovery UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL Tool Invocation
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      STATEMENT: In response to tools/list, the server shall return exactly the tool defined by this document for the approved GHOST prompt-engineering scope.

      The server shall not expose retrieval, memory, ingestion, blackboard, file-management, UI-control, answer-generation, or full-pipeline tools under this requirement set.
    GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL
    2.1.6. Tool Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-STATELESS-REQUEST-CONTRACT Stateless Prompt Request Contract
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-RATE-LIMITING Configurable MCP Tool Rate Limiting
    STATEMENT: The server shall accept tools/call for the declared GHOST prompt-engineering tool and shall execute the tool only after the request has passed protocol, authorization, and input validation.

    A tools/call request for an undeclared tool shall not execute GHOST application logic.
    • GHOST-MCP-STATELESS-REQUEST-CONTRACT
      2.1.8. Stateless Prompt Request Contract UID: GHOST-MCP-STATELESS-REQUEST-CONTRACT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL Tool Invocation
      STATEMENT: Each prompt-engineering tool call shall contain all input required to process that request.

      Correctness shall not depend on a previous MCP tool call, a previous client conversation turn, a server-side chat transcript, or a persistent MCP session object.

      Transport-level session identifiers may be supported by the selected MCP library, but the prompt-engineering result shall not depend on stored conversational state.
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-RATE-LIMITING
      2.2.8. Configurable MCP Tool Rate Limiting UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOL-RATE-LIMITING STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL Tool Invocation
      STATEMENT: The MCP server shall apply configurable rate limiting to authenticated invocations of ghost_engineer_prompt before creating a SuperPrompt or invoking deterministic PreProcessing or A2.

      When the configured limit is exceeded, the server shall reject the invocation without executing GHOST prompt-processing logic and shall return a standards-compatible, sanitized response that allows the client to retry according to the deployed policy.

      The concrete quota, time window, caller-identification key, retry behavior, and environment values shall remain deployment configuration owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc and its local deployment profile rather than being fixed in this interface contract.
    • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY
      2.1.1. ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility UID: GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      • GHOST-MCP-EXPLICIT-CLIENT-INVOCATION Explicit Client Invocation Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CROSS-CLIENT-CONSISTENCY Cross-Client Interface Consistency
      STATEMENT: The same GHOST MCP server implementation shall be interoperable with:

      - the owner's ChatGPT web client through its custom MCP plugin capability; and
      - Claude through its remote custom connector capability.

      Compatibility shall be demonstrated against both real clients and shall not be inferred only from an MCP Inspector or a unit-test client.
    GHOST-MCP-EXPLICIT-CLIENT-INVOCATION
    2.1.7. Explicit Client Invocation Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-EXPLICIT-CLIENT-INVOCATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
    STATEMENT: The MCP server shall operate when the user enables or explicitly invokes the GHOST plugin or connector in ChatGPT or Claude and the client issues the MCP tool call.

    The interface shall not claim that GHOST automatically receives every ordinary message written in a native ChatGPT or Claude conversation when no GHOST tool call occurs.
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL
      2.1.6. Tool Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-STATELESS-REQUEST-CONTRACT Stateless Prompt Request Contract
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-RATE-LIMITING Configurable MCP Tool Rate Limiting
      STATEMENT: The server shall accept tools/call for the declared GHOST prompt-engineering tool and shall execute the tool only after the request has passed protocol, authorization, and input validation.

      A tools/call request for an undeclared tool shall not execute GHOST application logic.
    GHOST-MCP-STATELESS-REQUEST-CONTRACT
    2.1.8. Stateless Prompt Request Contract UID: GHOST-MCP-STATELESS-REQUEST-CONTRACT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL Tool Invocation
    STATEMENT: Each prompt-engineering tool call shall contain all input required to process that request.

    Correctness shall not depend on a previous MCP tool call, a previous client conversation turn, a server-side chat transcript, or a persistent MCP session object.

    Transport-level session identifiers may be supported by the selected MCP library, but the prompt-engineering result shall not depend on stored conversational state.

    2.2. 2.2. Authorization and Transport Protection
    • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP
      2.1.2. Remote Streamable HTTP Transport UID: GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS External HTTPS Requirement
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
      • GHOST-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION Streamable HTTP Origin Validation
      STATEMENT: The GHOST MCP server shall expose the MCP protocol through Streamable HTTP.

      The server shall provide one logical MCP endpoint that supports the HTTP methods required by Streamable HTTP and shall exchange valid MCP JSON-RPC messages through that endpoint.

      The concrete public hostname, path, reverse-proxy configuration, container port, and AWS network realization shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS
    2.2.1. Protected Remote Access UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA OAuth Protected Resource Metadata
    • GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT Bearer Access Token Transport
    • GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION Access Token Resource Validation
    • GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE Authentication Failure Response
    • GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE Authorization Failure Response
    STATEMENT: The deployed remote MCP interface shall use the MCP HTTP authorization model based on OAuth 2.1 so that ChatGPT and Claude can obtain and present access tokens through their supported remote-MCP connection flows.

    The GHOST MCP server shall act as the protected resource server. Unauthenticated or invalidly authenticated requests shall not invoke PreProcessing, A2 PromptShaper, or any other GHOST application function.

    The authorization-server product, credential storage, client registration, discovery-route deployment, token-validation infrastructure, and environment values shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA
      2.2.2. OAuth Protected Resource Metadata UID: GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
      STATEMENT: The protected GHOST MCP endpoint shall publish OAuth Protected Resource Metadata through a standards-based discovery location supported by MCP clients.

      The metadata shall identify the canonical GHOST MCP resource and at least one authorization server approved by the deployment design.
    • GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT
      2.2.3. Bearer Access Token Transport UID: GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
      STATEMENT: An authorized MCP client shall present its access token in the HTTP Authorization header using the Bearer scheme on each protected MCP request.

      The MCP server shall not accept an access token from a URI query parameter.
    • GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION
      2.2.4. Access Token Resource Validation UID: GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
      STATEMENT: Before executing a protected MCP operation, the server shall validate that the presented token is active, unexpired, and issued for the canonical GHOST MCP resource.

      A token issued for another resource shall not authorize ghost_engineer_prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE
      2.2.5. Authentication Failure Response UID: GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
      STATEMENT: A request that requires authorization and presents no valid access token shall receive HTTP 401 and shall expose the standards-based discovery information required by the configured MCP authorization flow.

      The response shall not reveal secrets, token contents, internal exception traces, or GHOST prompt-processing data.
    • GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE
      2.2.6. Authorization Failure Response UID: GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
      STATEMENT: A request authenticated with credentials that do not authorize the requested MCP operation shall receive HTTP 403 and shall not execute the tool.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS
      2.2.1. Protected Remote Access UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA OAuth Protected Resource Metadata
      • GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT Bearer Access Token Transport
      • GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION Access Token Resource Validation
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE Authentication Failure Response
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE Authorization Failure Response
      STATEMENT: The deployed remote MCP interface shall use the MCP HTTP authorization model based on OAuth 2.1 so that ChatGPT and Claude can obtain and present access tokens through their supported remote-MCP connection flows.

      The GHOST MCP server shall act as the protected resource server. Unauthenticated or invalidly authenticated requests shall not invoke PreProcessing, A2 PromptShaper, or any other GHOST application function.

      The authorization-server product, credential storage, client registration, discovery-route deployment, token-validation infrastructure, and environment values shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA
    2.2.2. OAuth Protected Resource Metadata UID: GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
    STATEMENT: The protected GHOST MCP endpoint shall publish OAuth Protected Resource Metadata through a standards-based discovery location supported by MCP clients.

    The metadata shall identify the canonical GHOST MCP resource and at least one authorization server approved by the deployment design.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS
      2.2.1. Protected Remote Access UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA OAuth Protected Resource Metadata
      • GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT Bearer Access Token Transport
      • GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION Access Token Resource Validation
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE Authentication Failure Response
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE Authorization Failure Response
      STATEMENT: The deployed remote MCP interface shall use the MCP HTTP authorization model based on OAuth 2.1 so that ChatGPT and Claude can obtain and present access tokens through their supported remote-MCP connection flows.

      The GHOST MCP server shall act as the protected resource server. Unauthenticated or invalidly authenticated requests shall not invoke PreProcessing, A2 PromptShaper, or any other GHOST application function.

      The authorization-server product, credential storage, client registration, discovery-route deployment, token-validation infrastructure, and environment values shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT
    2.2.3. Bearer Access Token Transport UID: GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
    STATEMENT: An authorized MCP client shall present its access token in the HTTP Authorization header using the Bearer scheme on each protected MCP request.

    The MCP server shall not accept an access token from a URI query parameter.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS
      2.2.1. Protected Remote Access UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA OAuth Protected Resource Metadata
      • GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT Bearer Access Token Transport
      • GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION Access Token Resource Validation
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE Authentication Failure Response
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE Authorization Failure Response
      STATEMENT: The deployed remote MCP interface shall use the MCP HTTP authorization model based on OAuth 2.1 so that ChatGPT and Claude can obtain and present access tokens through their supported remote-MCP connection flows.

      The GHOST MCP server shall act as the protected resource server. Unauthenticated or invalidly authenticated requests shall not invoke PreProcessing, A2 PromptShaper, or any other GHOST application function.

      The authorization-server product, credential storage, client registration, discovery-route deployment, token-validation infrastructure, and environment values shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION
    2.2.4. Access Token Resource Validation UID: GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
    STATEMENT: Before executing a protected MCP operation, the server shall validate that the presented token is active, unexpired, and issued for the canonical GHOST MCP resource.

    A token issued for another resource shall not authorize ghost_engineer_prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS
      2.2.1. Protected Remote Access UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA OAuth Protected Resource Metadata
      • GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT Bearer Access Token Transport
      • GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION Access Token Resource Validation
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE Authentication Failure Response
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE Authorization Failure Response
      STATEMENT: The deployed remote MCP interface shall use the MCP HTTP authorization model based on OAuth 2.1 so that ChatGPT and Claude can obtain and present access tokens through their supported remote-MCP connection flows.

      The GHOST MCP server shall act as the protected resource server. Unauthenticated or invalidly authenticated requests shall not invoke PreProcessing, A2 PromptShaper, or any other GHOST application function.

      The authorization-server product, credential storage, client registration, discovery-route deployment, token-validation infrastructure, and environment values shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE
    2.2.5. Authentication Failure Response UID: GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
    STATEMENT: A request that requires authorization and presents no valid access token shall receive HTTP 401 and shall expose the standards-based discovery information required by the configured MCP authorization flow.

    The response shall not reveal secrets, token contents, internal exception traces, or GHOST prompt-processing data.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS
      2.2.1. Protected Remote Access UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE-METADATA OAuth Protected Resource Metadata
      • GHOST-MCP-BEARER-TOKEN-TRANSPORT Bearer Access Token Transport
      • GHOST-MCP-TOKEN-RESOURCE-VALIDATION Access Token Resource Validation
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHENTICATION-FAILURE Authentication Failure Response
      • GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE Authorization Failure Response
      STATEMENT: The deployed remote MCP interface shall use the MCP HTTP authorization model based on OAuth 2.1 so that ChatGPT and Claude can obtain and present access tokens through their supported remote-MCP connection flows.

      The GHOST MCP server shall act as the protected resource server. Unauthenticated or invalidly authenticated requests shall not invoke PreProcessing, A2 PromptShaper, or any other GHOST application function.

      The authorization-server product, credential storage, client registration, discovery-route deployment, token-validation infrastructure, and environment values shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE
    2.2.6. Authorization Failure Response UID: GHOST-MCP-AUTHORIZATION-FAILURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
    STATEMENT: A request authenticated with credentials that do not authorize the requested MCP operation shall receive HTTP 403 and shall not execute the tool.
    • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP
      2.1.2. Remote Streamable HTTP Transport UID: GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS External HTTPS Requirement
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTECTED-ACCESS Protected Remote Access
      • GHOST-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION Streamable HTTP Origin Validation
      STATEMENT: The GHOST MCP server shall expose the MCP protocol through Streamable HTTP.

      The server shall provide one logical MCP endpoint that supports the HTTP methods required by Streamable HTTP and shall exchange valid MCP JSON-RPC messages through that endpoint.

      The concrete public hostname, path, reverse-proxy configuration, container port, and AWS network realization shall remain owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc.
    GHOST-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION
    2.2.7. Streamable HTTP Origin Validation UID: GHOST-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
    STATEMENT: For each Streamable HTTP connection, the server shall validate the Origin header when the header is present.

    A present Origin value that is not accepted by the deployed configuration shall receive HTTP 403 before the request reaches GHOST prompt-processing logic.

    The exact accepted Origin values shall be established from verified ChatGPT and Claude client behavior and recorded in the deployment profile rather than invented in this interface document.
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL
      2.1.6. Tool Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-STATELESS-REQUEST-CONTRACT Stateless Prompt Request Contract
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-RATE-LIMITING Configurable MCP Tool Rate Limiting
      STATEMENT: The server shall accept tools/call for the declared GHOST prompt-engineering tool and shall execute the tool only after the request has passed protocol, authorization, and input validation.

      A tools/call request for an undeclared tool shall not execute GHOST application logic.
    GHOST-MCP-TOOL-RATE-LIMITING
    2.2.8. Configurable MCP Tool Rate Limiting UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOL-RATE-LIMITING STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL Tool Invocation
    STATEMENT: The MCP server shall apply configurable rate limiting to authenticated invocations of ghost_engineer_prompt before creating a SuperPrompt or invoking deterministic PreProcessing or A2.

    When the configured limit is exceeded, the server shall reject the invocation without executing GHOST prompt-processing logic and shall return a standards-compatible, sanitized response that allows the client to retry according to the deployed policy.

    The concrete quota, time window, caller-identification key, retry behavior, and environment values shall remain deployment configuration owned by 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc and its local deployment profile rather than being fixed in this interface contract.

    3. 3. Prompt-Engineering Capability

    3.1. 3.1. MCP Tool Definition
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST
      2.1.5. Tool Discovery UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-INITIALIZATION MCP Initialization and Capability Negotiation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-CALL Tool Invocation
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      STATEMENT: In response to tools/list, the server shall return exactly the tool defined by this document for the approved GHOST prompt-engineering scope.

      The server shall not expose retrieval, memory, ingestion, blackboard, file-management, UI-control, answer-generation, or full-pipeline tools under this requirement set.
    GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL
    3.1.1. GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE Prompt-Engineering Tool Display Title
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION Prompt-Engineering Tool Description
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS Prompt-Engineering Tool Behavioral Annotations
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
    • GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT No Project or Memory Persistence Side Effect
    STATEMENT: The MCP server shall expose one tool with the stable identifier:

    ghost_engineer_prompt

    The tool shall transform one supplied prompt by executing existing GHOST PreProcessing followed by existing A2 PromptShaper and shall return the resulting engineered prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE
      3.1.2. Prompt-Engineering Tool Display Title UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      STATEMENT: The human-readable title of ghost_engineer_prompt shall identify the capability as GHOST Prompt Engineering and shall not imply retrieval, memory, answer generation, or complete pipeline execution.
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION
      3.1.3. Prompt-Engineering Tool Description UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      STATEMENT: The tool description presented through tools/list shall state that the tool:

      - accepts a user prompt;
      - runs GHOST PreProcessing and A2 PromptShaper;
      - returns the engineered prompt;
      - does not answer the original prompt; and
      - is intended to present the engineered prompt back to the user.

      The description shall not claim execution of capabilities excluded by this document.
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS
      3.1.4. Prompt-Engineering Tool Behavioral Annotations UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      STATEMENT: The tool metadata shall identify ghost_engineer_prompt as non-destructive and non-persistent with respect to GHOST project data and memory data.

      The metadata shall not describe the tool as modifying files, projects, memories, blackboards, client conversations, or external business records.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA
      3.2.1. Prompt Input Schema UID: GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY Non-Empty Prompt Input
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-UNICODE Unicode Prompt Preservation
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-CONVERSATION-PAYLOAD No Conversation-History Input
      STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt inputSchema shall be a JSON Schema object whose root type is object and that has exactly one supported property:

      - prompt_text: string containing the prompt to be engineered.

      prompt_text shall be required. additionalProperties shall be false.
    • GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT
      3.4.6. No Project or Memory Persistence Side Effect UID: GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      STATEMENT: Execution of ghost_engineer_prompt shall not create, modify, ingest, retrieve, or delete GHOST project documents, Chroma data, memory files, memory indexes, ActiveBrief files, blackboard records, or client conversation records.

      Any external API usage required by existing A2 execution does not change this project-data and memory-data boundary.
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL
      3.1.1. GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE Prompt-Engineering Tool Display Title
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION Prompt-Engineering Tool Description
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS Prompt-Engineering Tool Behavioral Annotations
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT No Project or Memory Persistence Side Effect
      STATEMENT: The MCP server shall expose one tool with the stable identifier:

      ghost_engineer_prompt

      The tool shall transform one supplied prompt by executing existing GHOST PreProcessing followed by existing A2 PromptShaper and shall return the resulting engineered prompt.
    GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE
    3.1.2. Prompt-Engineering Tool Display Title UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
    STATEMENT: The human-readable title of ghost_engineer_prompt shall identify the capability as GHOST Prompt Engineering and shall not imply retrieval, memory, answer generation, or complete pipeline execution.
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL
      3.1.1. GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE Prompt-Engineering Tool Display Title
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION Prompt-Engineering Tool Description
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS Prompt-Engineering Tool Behavioral Annotations
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT No Project or Memory Persistence Side Effect
      STATEMENT: The MCP server shall expose one tool with the stable identifier:

      ghost_engineer_prompt

      The tool shall transform one supplied prompt by executing existing GHOST PreProcessing followed by existing A2 PromptShaper and shall return the resulting engineered prompt.
    GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION
    3.1.3. Prompt-Engineering Tool Description UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
    STATEMENT: The tool description presented through tools/list shall state that the tool:

    - accepts a user prompt;
    - runs GHOST PreProcessing and A2 PromptShaper;
    - returns the engineered prompt;
    - does not answer the original prompt; and
    - is intended to present the engineered prompt back to the user.

    The description shall not claim execution of capabilities excluded by this document.
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL
      3.1.1. GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE Prompt-Engineering Tool Display Title
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION Prompt-Engineering Tool Description
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS Prompt-Engineering Tool Behavioral Annotations
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT No Project or Memory Persistence Side Effect
      STATEMENT: The MCP server shall expose one tool with the stable identifier:

      ghost_engineer_prompt

      The tool shall transform one supplied prompt by executing existing GHOST PreProcessing followed by existing A2 PromptShaper and shall return the resulting engineered prompt.
    GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS
    3.1.4. Prompt-Engineering Tool Behavioral Annotations UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
    STATEMENT: The tool metadata shall identify ghost_engineer_prompt as non-destructive and non-persistent with respect to GHOST project data and memory data.

    The metadata shall not describe the tool as modifying files, projects, memories, blackboards, client conversations, or external business records.

    3.2. 3.2. Input Contract
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL
      3.1.1. GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE Prompt-Engineering Tool Display Title
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION Prompt-Engineering Tool Description
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS Prompt-Engineering Tool Behavioral Annotations
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT No Project or Memory Persistence Side Effect
      STATEMENT: The MCP server shall expose one tool with the stable identifier:

      ghost_engineer_prompt

      The tool shall transform one supplied prompt by executing existing GHOST PreProcessing followed by existing A2 PromptShaper and shall return the resulting engineered prompt.
    GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA
    3.2.1. Prompt Input Schema UID: GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY Non-Empty Prompt Input
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-UNICODE Unicode Prompt Preservation
    • GHOST-MCP-NO-CONVERSATION-PAYLOAD No Conversation-History Input
    STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt inputSchema shall be a JSON Schema object whose root type is object and that has exactly one supported property:

    - prompt_text: string containing the prompt to be engineered.

    prompt_text shall be required. additionalProperties shall be false.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY
      3.2.2. Non-Empty Prompt Input UID: GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-INPUT-VALIDATION-ERROR Prompt Validation Tool Error
      STATEMENT: The server shall reject prompt_text when its value is empty or contains only whitespace.

      The validation check may inspect a trimmed view of the string, but a valid prompt shall be passed to the existing PreProcessing function as the user supplied it rather than being rewritten by the MCP adapter.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-UNICODE
      3.2.3. Unicode Prompt Preservation UID: GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-UNICODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
      STATEMENT: The MCP adapter shall accept valid Unicode prompt text and shall preserve the prompt content, line breaks, Markdown headings, and language characters when passing the value into existing GHOST PreProcessing.

      Any normalization performed inside PreProcessing shall remain governed by 30_preprocessing_prompt_functions.sdoc rather than by the MCP adapter.
    • GHOST-MCP-NO-CONVERSATION-PAYLOAD
      3.2.4. No Conversation-History Input UID: GHOST-MCP-NO-CONVERSATION-PAYLOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
      STATEMENT: The approved ghost_engineer_prompt schema shall not require or accept previous assistant responses, full conversation history, memory records, document chunks, project identifiers, blackboard entries, or retrieval instructions.

      Those inputs shall not be added without separately approved requirements and a deliberate interface-version decision.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA
      3.2.1. Prompt Input Schema UID: GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY Non-Empty Prompt Input
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-UNICODE Unicode Prompt Preservation
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-CONVERSATION-PAYLOAD No Conversation-History Input
      STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt inputSchema shall be a JSON Schema object whose root type is object and that has exactly one supported property:

      - prompt_text: string containing the prompt to be engineered.

      prompt_text shall be required. additionalProperties shall be false.
    GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY
    3.2.2. Non-Empty Prompt Input UID: GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-INPUT-VALIDATION-ERROR Prompt Validation Tool Error
    STATEMENT: The server shall reject prompt_text when its value is empty or contains only whitespace.

    The validation check may inspect a trimmed view of the string, but a valid prompt shall be passed to the existing PreProcessing function as the user supplied it rather than being rewritten by the MCP adapter.
    • GHOST-MCP-INPUT-VALIDATION-ERROR
      4.1.2. Prompt Validation Tool Error UID: GHOST-MCP-INPUT-VALIDATION-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY Non-Empty Prompt Input
      STATEMENT: A structurally valid tools/call request whose arguments fail the ghost_engineer_prompt input contract shall return a CallToolResult with isError true and a concise actionable message that allows the client model to correct the tool call.

      This includes a missing prompt_text property, a non-string prompt_text value, an unsupported additional property, or a prompt_text value that is empty or whitespace-only after validation.

      The server shall not call deterministic PreProcessing or A2 for that request.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA
      3.2.1. Prompt Input Schema UID: GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY Non-Empty Prompt Input
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-UNICODE Unicode Prompt Preservation
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-CONVERSATION-PAYLOAD No Conversation-History Input
      STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt inputSchema shall be a JSON Schema object whose root type is object and that has exactly one supported property:

      - prompt_text: string containing the prompt to be engineered.

      prompt_text shall be required. additionalProperties shall be false.
    GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-UNICODE
    3.2.3. Unicode Prompt Preservation UID: GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-UNICODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
    STATEMENT: The MCP adapter shall accept valid Unicode prompt text and shall preserve the prompt content, line breaks, Markdown headings, and language characters when passing the value into existing GHOST PreProcessing.

    Any normalization performed inside PreProcessing shall remain governed by 30_preprocessing_prompt_functions.sdoc rather than by the MCP adapter.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA
      3.2.1. Prompt Input Schema UID: GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY Non-Empty Prompt Input
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-UNICODE Unicode Prompt Preservation
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-CONVERSATION-PAYLOAD No Conversation-History Input
      STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt inputSchema shall be a JSON Schema object whose root type is object and that has exactly one supported property:

      - prompt_text: string containing the prompt to be engineered.

      prompt_text shall be required. additionalProperties shall be false.
    GHOST-MCP-NO-CONVERSATION-PAYLOAD
    3.2.4. No Conversation-History Input UID: GHOST-MCP-NO-CONVERSATION-PAYLOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
    STATEMENT: The approved ghost_engineer_prompt schema shall not require or accept previous assistant responses, full conversation history, memory records, document chunks, project identifiers, blackboard entries, or retrieval instructions.

    Those inputs shall not be added without separately approved requirements and a deliberate interface-version decision.

    3.3. 3.3. Processing Sequence
    • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW
      1.2. Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow UID: GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call
      • GHOST-MCP-EXISTING-PROMPT-SCHEMA Existing Prompt Schema Reuse
      STATEMENT: The MCP prompt-engineering flow shall be exactly:

      1. receive one user-supplied prompt;
      2. create one fresh SuperPrompt for that request;
      3. execute the existing deterministic PreProcessing function on that SuperPrompt;
      4. execute the existing A2 PromptShaper on the same SuperPrompt;
      5. obtain the engineered prompt from the final SuperPrompt prompt-ready projection; and
      6. return that engineered prompt to the invoking MCP client.

      No additional GHOST pipeline stage shall be inserted into this flow by this interface.
    GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT
    3.3.1. Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call UID: GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL Existing PreProcessing Invocation
    • GHOST-MCP-REQUEST-ISOLATION Concurrent Request Isolation
    STATEMENT: The MCP adapter shall create one new SuperPrompt for each accepted ghost_engineer_prompt call.

    It shall not reuse a mutable SuperPrompt from another request, another client, another user, or an earlier tool call.
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL
      3.3.3. Existing PreProcessing Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE PreProcessing Completion Gate
      STATEMENT: The adapter shall pass prompt_text, the fresh SuperPrompt, and the active PromptSchema to the existing deterministic PreProcessing function.

      The adapter shall not reproduce Markdown parsing, header matching, TASK/CONTEXT fallback, mandatory-field handling, retrieval-query construction, stage update, or prompt-ready composition in parallel MCP-specific code.
    • GHOST-MCP-REQUEST-ISOLATION
      3.3.10. Concurrent Request Isolation UID: GHOST-MCP-REQUEST-ISOLATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call
      STATEMENT: Concurrent MCP calls shall not share mutable SuperPrompt state, A2 selected-result state, prompt-ready output, or request-specific exception state.

      The engineered prompt returned for one call shall be derived only from that call's prompt_text and the shared read-only configuration used by PreProcessing and A2.
    • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW
      1.2. Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow UID: GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call
      • GHOST-MCP-EXISTING-PROMPT-SCHEMA Existing Prompt Schema Reuse
      STATEMENT: The MCP prompt-engineering flow shall be exactly:

      1. receive one user-supplied prompt;
      2. create one fresh SuperPrompt for that request;
      3. execute the existing deterministic PreProcessing function on that SuperPrompt;
      4. execute the existing A2 PromptShaper on the same SuperPrompt;
      5. obtain the engineered prompt from the final SuperPrompt prompt-ready projection; and
      6. return that engineered prompt to the invoking MCP client.

      No additional GHOST pipeline stage shall be inserted into this flow by this interface.
    GHOST-MCP-EXISTING-PROMPT-SCHEMA
    3.3.2. Existing Prompt Schema Reuse UID: GHOST-MCP-EXISTING-PROMPT-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
    STATEMENT: The MCP adapter shall use the same active PromptSchema configuration used by the GHOST application for deterministic PreProcessing.

    The adapter shall not define a second MCP-specific canonical-header vocabulary, mandatory-field set, alias map, bilingual map, or default map.
    • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT
      3.3.1. Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call UID: GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL Existing PreProcessing Invocation
      • GHOST-MCP-REQUEST-ISOLATION Concurrent Request Isolation
      STATEMENT: The MCP adapter shall create one new SuperPrompt for each accepted ghost_engineer_prompt call.

      It shall not reuse a mutable SuperPrompt from another request, another client, another user, or an earlier tool call.
    GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL
    3.3.3. Existing PreProcessing Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
