    • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE PreProcessing Completion Gate
    STATEMENT: The adapter shall pass prompt_text, the fresh SuperPrompt, and the active PromptSchema to the existing deterministic PreProcessing function.

    The adapter shall not reproduce Markdown parsing, header matching, TASK/CONTEXT fallback, mandatory-field handling, retrieval-query construction, stage update, or prompt-ready composition in parallel MCP-specific code.
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE
      3.3.4. PreProcessing Completion Gate UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL Existing PreProcessing Invocation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-EXECUTION-ERROR PreProcessing Execution Error
      STATEMENT: A2 PromptShaper shall be invoked only after PreProcessing has completed successfully on the same SuperPrompt.

      If PreProcessing fails, the adapter shall stop the processing path and shall not call A2.
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL
      3.3.3. Existing PreProcessing Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE PreProcessing Completion Gate
      STATEMENT: The adapter shall pass prompt_text, the fresh SuperPrompt, and the active PromptSchema to the existing deterministic PreProcessing function.

      The adapter shall not reproduce Markdown parsing, header matching, TASK/CONTEXT fallback, mandatory-field handling, retrieval-query construction, stage update, or prompt-ready composition in parallel MCP-specific code.
    GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE
    3.3.4. PreProcessing Completion Gate UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL Existing PreProcessing Invocation
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-EXECUTION-ERROR PreProcessing Execution Error
    STATEMENT: A2 PromptShaper shall be invoked only after PreProcessing has completed successfully on the same SuperPrompt.

    If PreProcessing fails, the adapter shall stop the processing path and shall not call A2.
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL
      3.3.5. Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE PreProcessing Completion Gate
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Execution Mode
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE A2 Completion Gate
      STATEMENT: After successful PreProcessing, the adapter shall invoke the existing A2 PromptShaper on the same SuperPrompt using the existing AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, LLMClient, configured A2 agent definition, and configured model settings.

      The MCP adapter shall not hard-code a second A2 prompt, option catalog, model name, temperature, token limit, selector parser, sanitizer, or field-write-back implementation.
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-EXECUTION-ERROR
      4.1.3. PreProcessing Execution Error UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-EXECUTION-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE PreProcessing Completion Gate
      STATEMENT: If existing PreProcessing raises an exception or fails to produce the required prepared state, the server shall return a tool execution result with isError true, shall not invoke A2, and shall not return a partial engineered prompt as a successful result.
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE
      3.3.4. PreProcessing Completion Gate UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL Existing PreProcessing Invocation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-EXECUTION-ERROR PreProcessing Execution Error
      STATEMENT: A2 PromptShaper shall be invoked only after PreProcessing has completed successfully on the same SuperPrompt.

      If PreProcessing fails, the adapter shall stop the processing path and shall not call A2.
    GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL
    3.3.5. Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE PreProcessing Completion Gate
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Execution Mode
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE A2 Completion Gate
    STATEMENT: After successful PreProcessing, the adapter shall invoke the existing A2 PromptShaper on the same SuperPrompt using the existing AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, LLMClient, configured A2 agent definition, and configured model settings.

    The MCP adapter shall not hard-code a second A2 prompt, option catalog, model name, temperature, token limit, selector parser, sanitizer, or field-write-back implementation.
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-LLM-MODE
      3.3.6. A2 LLM Execution Mode UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-LLM-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation
      STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt path shall execute A2 in its configured LLM mode so that A2 evaluates the preprocessed request and updates the configured prompt meta-fields.

      A deterministic-default-only A2 path shall not be substituted silently for a requested normal tool execution.
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE
      3.3.7. A2 Completion Gate UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-EXECUTION-ERROR A2 Execution Error
      STATEMENT: A successful ghost_engineer_prompt result shall be returned only after A2 has completed, the SuperPrompt stage is a2, and the central prompt-ready projection has been rebuilt from the updated SuperPrompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL
      3.3.5. Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE PreProcessing Completion Gate
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Execution Mode
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE A2 Completion Gate
      STATEMENT: After successful PreProcessing, the adapter shall invoke the existing A2 PromptShaper on the same SuperPrompt using the existing AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, LLMClient, configured A2 agent definition, and configured model settings.

      The MCP adapter shall not hard-code a second A2 prompt, option catalog, model name, temperature, token limit, selector parser, sanitizer, or field-write-back implementation.
    GHOST-MCP-A2-LLM-MODE
    3.3.6. A2 LLM Execution Mode UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-LLM-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation
    STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt path shall execute A2 in its configured LLM mode so that A2 evaluates the preprocessed request and updates the configured prompt meta-fields.

    A deterministic-default-only A2 path shall not be substituted silently for a requested normal tool execution.
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL
      3.3.5. Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE PreProcessing Completion Gate
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Execution Mode
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE A2 Completion Gate
      STATEMENT: After successful PreProcessing, the adapter shall invoke the existing A2 PromptShaper on the same SuperPrompt using the existing AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, LLMClient, configured A2 agent definition, and configured model settings.

      The MCP adapter shall not hard-code a second A2 prompt, option catalog, model name, temperature, token limit, selector parser, sanitizer, or field-write-back implementation.
    GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE
    3.3.7. A2 Completion Gate UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-EXECUTION-ERROR A2 Execution Error
    STATEMENT: A successful ghost_engineer_prompt result shall be returned only after A2 has completed, the SuperPrompt stage is a2, and the central prompt-ready projection has been rebuilt from the updated SuperPrompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE
      3.3.8. Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE A2 Completion Gate
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION No Original-Request Answer Generation
      • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
      STATEMENT: The engineered prompt returned by ghost_engineer_prompt shall be the final non-empty SuperPrompt prompt_ready value produced after A2 completion.

      The MCP adapter shall not independently reformat, summarize, paraphrase, decorate, or regenerate that engineered prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-EXECUTION-ERROR
      4.1.4. A2 Execution Error UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-EXECUTION-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE A2 Completion Gate
      STATEMENT: If A2 configuration loading, LLM invocation, response parsing, selector validation, or final projection fails, the server shall return a tool execution result with isError true and shall not present the pre-A2 prompt preview as the completed engineered prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE
      3.3.7. A2 Completion Gate UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-EXECUTION-ERROR A2 Execution Error
      STATEMENT: A successful ghost_engineer_prompt result shall be returned only after A2 has completed, the SuperPrompt stage is a2, and the central prompt-ready projection has been rebuilt from the updated SuperPrompt.
    GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE
    3.3.8. Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE A2 Completion Gate
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION No Original-Request Answer Generation
    • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
    STATEMENT: The engineered prompt returned by ghost_engineer_prompt shall be the final non-empty SuperPrompt prompt_ready value produced after A2 completion.

    The MCP adapter shall not independently reformat, summarize, paraphrase, decorate, or regenerate that engineered prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION
      3.3.9. No Original-Request Answer Generation UID: GHOST-MCP-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
      STATEMENT: The MCP server shall not use the engineered prompt to generate an answer to the user's original request within ghost_engineer_prompt.

      The successful tool result shall contain the engineered prompt itself. Any later use of that engineered prompt by ChatGPT, Claude, the user, or another GHOST capability shall occur outside this tool execution.
    • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT
      3.4.1. Successful Tool Result UID: GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA Structured Output Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-BACKWARD-COMPATIBILITY Text and Structured Result Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-PREFIX-SUFFIX No Adapter-Generated Prompt Wrapping
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-INTERNALS No Internal Runtime Disclosure
      STATEMENT: On success, ghost_engineer_prompt shall return an MCP CallToolResult with:

      - isError omitted or false;
      - one TextContent item whose text is exactly the engineered prompt; and
      - structuredContent conforming to GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA.
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE
      3.3.8. Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE A2 Completion Gate
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION No Original-Request Answer Generation
      • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
      STATEMENT: The engineered prompt returned by ghost_engineer_prompt shall be the final non-empty SuperPrompt prompt_ready value produced after A2 completion.

      The MCP adapter shall not independently reformat, summarize, paraphrase, decorate, or regenerate that engineered prompt.
    GHOST-MCP-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION
    3.3.9. No Original-Request Answer Generation UID: GHOST-MCP-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
    STATEMENT: The MCP server shall not use the engineered prompt to generate an answer to the user's original request within ghost_engineer_prompt.

    The successful tool result shall contain the engineered prompt itself. Any later use of that engineered prompt by ChatGPT, Claude, the user, or another GHOST capability shall occur outside this tool execution.
    • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT
      3.3.1. Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call UID: GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL Existing PreProcessing Invocation
      • GHOST-MCP-REQUEST-ISOLATION Concurrent Request Isolation
      STATEMENT: The MCP adapter shall create one new SuperPrompt for each accepted ghost_engineer_prompt call.

      It shall not reuse a mutable SuperPrompt from another request, another client, another user, or an earlier tool call.
    GHOST-MCP-REQUEST-ISOLATION
    3.3.10. Concurrent Request Isolation UID: GHOST-MCP-REQUEST-ISOLATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-FRESH-SUPERPROMPT Fresh SuperPrompt Per Tool Call
    STATEMENT: Concurrent MCP calls shall not share mutable SuperPrompt state, A2 selected-result state, prompt-ready output, or request-specific exception state.

    The engineered prompt returned for one call shall be derived only from that call's prompt_text and the shared read-only configuration used by PreProcessing and A2.

    3.4. 3.4. Output Contract
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE
      3.3.8. Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE A2 Completion Gate
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-ANSWER-GENERATION No Original-Request Answer Generation
      • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
      STATEMENT: The engineered prompt returned by ghost_engineer_prompt shall be the final non-empty SuperPrompt prompt_ready value produced after A2 completion.

      The MCP adapter shall not independently reformat, summarize, paraphrase, decorate, or regenerate that engineered prompt.
    GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT
    3.4.1. Successful Tool Result UID: GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA Structured Output Schema
    • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-BACKWARD-COMPATIBILITY Text and Structured Result Compatibility
    • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-PREFIX-SUFFIX No Adapter-Generated Prompt Wrapping
    • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-INTERNALS No Internal Runtime Disclosure
    STATEMENT: On success, ghost_engineer_prompt shall return an MCP CallToolResult with:

    - isError omitted or false;
    - one TextContent item whose text is exactly the engineered prompt; and
    - structuredContent conforming to GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA.
    • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA
      3.4.2. Structured Output Schema UID: GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
      STATEMENT: The tool outputSchema shall be a JSON Schema object whose root type is object, and successful structuredContent shall define exactly these required properties:

      - engineered_prompt: non-empty string equal to the TextContent value and to the final SuperPrompt prompt_ready value;
      - stage: string with the value a2.

      additionalProperties shall be false.
    • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-BACKWARD-COMPATIBILITY
      3.4.3. Text and Structured Result Compatibility UID: GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-BACKWARD-COMPATIBILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
      STATEMENT: The server shall return the engineered prompt in TextContent even when structuredContent is also returned, so that clients that do not render structured output can still receive and display the engineered prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-PREFIX-SUFFIX
      3.4.4. No Adapter-Generated Prompt Wrapping UID: GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-PREFIX-SUFFIX STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
      STATEMENT: The TextContent value shall not add an MCP-specific introduction, explanation, success message, Markdown fence, client instruction, or trailing commentary around the engineered prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-INTERNALS
      3.4.5. No Internal Runtime Disclosure UID: GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-INTERNALS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
      STATEMENT: A successful result shall not expose the OpenAI API key, authorization tokens, full A2 internal prompt, raw A2 model response, stack traces, configuration secrets, server filesystem paths, or unrelated SuperPrompt runtime objects.
    • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT
      3.4.1. Successful Tool Result UID: GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA Structured Output Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-BACKWARD-COMPATIBILITY Text and Structured Result Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-PREFIX-SUFFIX No Adapter-Generated Prompt Wrapping
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-INTERNALS No Internal Runtime Disclosure
      STATEMENT: On success, ghost_engineer_prompt shall return an MCP CallToolResult with:

      - isError omitted or false;
      - one TextContent item whose text is exactly the engineered prompt; and
      - structuredContent conforming to GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA.
    GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA
    3.4.2. Structured Output Schema UID: GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
    STATEMENT: The tool outputSchema shall be a JSON Schema object whose root type is object, and successful structuredContent shall define exactly these required properties:

    - engineered_prompt: non-empty string equal to the TextContent value and to the final SuperPrompt prompt_ready value;
    - stage: string with the value a2.

    additionalProperties shall be false.
    • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT
      3.4.1. Successful Tool Result UID: GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA Structured Output Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-BACKWARD-COMPATIBILITY Text and Structured Result Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-PREFIX-SUFFIX No Adapter-Generated Prompt Wrapping
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-INTERNALS No Internal Runtime Disclosure
      STATEMENT: On success, ghost_engineer_prompt shall return an MCP CallToolResult with:

      - isError omitted or false;
      - one TextContent item whose text is exactly the engineered prompt; and
      - structuredContent conforming to GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA.
    GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-BACKWARD-COMPATIBILITY
    3.4.3. Text and Structured Result Compatibility UID: GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-BACKWARD-COMPATIBILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
    STATEMENT: The server shall return the engineered prompt in TextContent even when structuredContent is also returned, so that clients that do not render structured output can still receive and display the engineered prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT
      3.4.1. Successful Tool Result UID: GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA Structured Output Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-BACKWARD-COMPATIBILITY Text and Structured Result Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-PREFIX-SUFFIX No Adapter-Generated Prompt Wrapping
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-INTERNALS No Internal Runtime Disclosure
      STATEMENT: On success, ghost_engineer_prompt shall return an MCP CallToolResult with:

      - isError omitted or false;
      - one TextContent item whose text is exactly the engineered prompt; and
      - structuredContent conforming to GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA.
    GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-PREFIX-SUFFIX
    3.4.4. No Adapter-Generated Prompt Wrapping UID: GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-PREFIX-SUFFIX STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
    STATEMENT: The TextContent value shall not add an MCP-specific introduction, explanation, success message, Markdown fence, client instruction, or trailing commentary around the engineered prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT
      3.4.1. Successful Tool Result UID: GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA Structured Output Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-BACKWARD-COMPATIBILITY Text and Structured Result Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-PREFIX-SUFFIX No Adapter-Generated Prompt Wrapping
      • GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-INTERNALS No Internal Runtime Disclosure
      STATEMENT: On success, ghost_engineer_prompt shall return an MCP CallToolResult with:

      - isError omitted or false;
      - one TextContent item whose text is exactly the engineered prompt; and
      - structuredContent conforming to GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-SCHEMA.
    GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-INTERNALS
    3.4.5. No Internal Runtime Disclosure UID: GHOST-MCP-OUTPUT-NO-INTERNALS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-SUCCESS-RESULT Successful Tool Result
    STATEMENT: A successful result shall not expose the OpenAI API key, authorization tokens, full A2 internal prompt, raw A2 model response, stack traces, configuration secrets, server filesystem paths, or unrelated SuperPrompt runtime objects.
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL
      3.1.1. GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool UID: GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOLS-LIST Tool Discovery
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TITLE Prompt-Engineering Tool Display Title
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-DESCRIPTION Prompt-Engineering Tool Description
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-SAFETY-ANNOTATIONS Prompt-Engineering Tool Behavioral Annotations
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT No Project or Memory Persistence Side Effect
      STATEMENT: The MCP server shall expose one tool with the stable identifier:

      ghost_engineer_prompt

      The tool shall transform one supplied prompt by executing existing GHOST PreProcessing followed by existing A2 PromptShaper and shall return the resulting engineered prompt.
    GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT
    3.4.6. No Project or Memory Persistence Side Effect UID: GHOST-MCP-NO-PERSISTENCE-SIDE-EFFECT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEER-PROMPT-TOOL GHOST Prompt-Engineering Tool
    STATEMENT: Execution of ghost_engineer_prompt shall not create, modify, ingest, retrieve, or delete GHOST project documents, Chroma data, memory files, memory indexes, ActiveBrief files, blackboard records, or client conversation records.

    Any external API usage required by existing A2 execution does not change this project-data and memory-data boundary.

    4. 4. Interface Data, Errors, and Acceptance

    4.1. 4.1. Error Contract
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT
      1.1. GHOST MCP Interface Scope UID: GHOST-MCP-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Interface Family Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY Product and Python Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR Malformed MCP Request Error
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one remote MCP interface that allows ChatGPT and Claude to submit a user prompt, execute the approved GHOST prompt-engineering path, and receive the resulting engineered prompt in the same client conversation.

      This requirement defines a target interface. The MCP capability shall not be represented as implemented until the server code and the real-client acceptance tests defined by this document have passed.
    GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR
    4.1.1. Malformed MCP Request Error UID: GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-ERROR-SANITIZATION Error Information Sanitization
    STATEMENT: A malformed JSON-RPC request, an unsupported MCP method, an unknown tool name, or a request that fails the structural MCP CallToolRequest contract shall be reported through the MCP protocol error mechanism and shall not invoke GHOST prompt-processing logic.

    Tool-specific argument validation failures shall not be classified as malformed MCP requests solely because the supplied arguments do not satisfy the ghost_engineer_prompt inputSchema.
    • GHOST-MCP-ERROR-SANITIZATION
      4.1.5. Error Information Sanitization UID: GHOST-MCP-ERROR-SANITIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR Malformed MCP Request Error
      STATEMENT: Tool execution errors shall identify the failed phase sufficiently for client-visible diagnosis without returning secret values, bearer tokens, raw upstream model responses, complete internal prompts, stack traces, or server-local filesystem details.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY
      3.2.2. Non-Empty Prompt Input UID: GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-SCHEMA Prompt Input Schema
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-INPUT-VALIDATION-ERROR Prompt Validation Tool Error
      STATEMENT: The server shall reject prompt_text when its value is empty or contains only whitespace.

      The validation check may inspect a trimmed view of the string, but a valid prompt shall be passed to the existing PreProcessing function as the user supplied it rather than being rewritten by the MCP adapter.
    GHOST-MCP-INPUT-VALIDATION-ERROR
    4.1.2. Prompt Validation Tool Error UID: GHOST-MCP-INPUT-VALIDATION-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROMPT-INPUT-NONEMPTY Non-Empty Prompt Input
    STATEMENT: A structurally valid tools/call request whose arguments fail the ghost_engineer_prompt input contract shall return a CallToolResult with isError true and a concise actionable message that allows the client model to correct the tool call.

    This includes a missing prompt_text property, a non-string prompt_text value, an unsupported additional property, or a prompt_text value that is empty or whitespace-only after validation.

    The server shall not call deterministic PreProcessing or A2 for that request.
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE
      3.3.4. PreProcessing Completion Gate UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-CALL Existing PreProcessing Invocation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-EXECUTION-ERROR PreProcessing Execution Error
      STATEMENT: A2 PromptShaper shall be invoked only after PreProcessing has completed successfully on the same SuperPrompt.

      If PreProcessing fails, the adapter shall stop the processing path and shall not call A2.
    GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-EXECUTION-ERROR
    4.1.3. PreProcessing Execution Error UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-EXECUTION-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESS-STAGE-GATE PreProcessing Completion Gate
    STATEMENT: If existing PreProcessing raises an exception or fails to produce the required prepared state, the server shall return a tool execution result with isError true, shall not invoke A2, and shall not return a partial engineered prompt as a successful result.
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE
      3.3.7. A2 Completion Gate UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-CALL Existing A2 PromptShaper Invocation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ENGINEERED-PROMPT-SOURCE Authoritative Engineered Prompt Source
      • GHOST-MCP-A2-EXECUTION-ERROR A2 Execution Error
      STATEMENT: A successful ghost_engineer_prompt result shall be returned only after A2 has completed, the SuperPrompt stage is a2, and the central prompt-ready projection has been rebuilt from the updated SuperPrompt.
    GHOST-MCP-A2-EXECUTION-ERROR
    4.1.4. A2 Execution Error UID: GHOST-MCP-A2-EXECUTION-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-A2-COMPLETION-GATE A2 Completion Gate
    STATEMENT: If A2 configuration loading, LLM invocation, response parsing, selector validation, or final projection fails, the server shall return a tool execution result with isError true and shall not present the pre-A2 prompt preview as the completed engineered prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR
      4.1.1. Malformed MCP Request Error UID: GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-ERROR-SANITIZATION Error Information Sanitization
      STATEMENT: A malformed JSON-RPC request, an unsupported MCP method, an unknown tool name, or a request that fails the structural MCP CallToolRequest contract shall be reported through the MCP protocol error mechanism and shall not invoke GHOST prompt-processing logic.

      Tool-specific argument validation failures shall not be classified as malformed MCP requests solely because the supplied arguments do not satisfy the ghost_engineer_prompt inputSchema.
    GHOST-MCP-ERROR-SANITIZATION
    4.1.5. Error Information Sanitization UID: GHOST-MCP-ERROR-SANITIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR Malformed MCP Request Error
    STATEMENT: Tool execution errors shall identify the failed phase sufficiently for client-visible diagnosis without returning secret values, bearer tokens, raw upstream model responses, complete internal prompts, stack traces, or server-local filesystem details.

    4.2. 4.2. Protocol Acceptance
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT
      1.1. GHOST MCP Interface Scope UID: GHOST-MCP-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Interface Family Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY Product and Python Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR Malformed MCP Request Error
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one remote MCP interface that allows ChatGPT and Claude to submit a user prompt, execute the approved GHOST prompt-engineering path, and receive the resulting engineered prompt in the same client conversation.

      This requirement defines a target interface. The MCP capability shall not be represented as implemented until the server code and the real-client acceptance tests defined by this document have passed.
    GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
    4.2.1. MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE Exact Tool Inventory Acceptance
    • GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE PreProcessing-before-A2 Acceptance
    • GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE Concurrent Isolation Acceptance
    • GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE ChatGPT End-to-End Acceptance
    • GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE Claude End-to-End Acceptance
    STATEMENT: The server shall pass an acceptance sequence that performs, in order:

    1. initialize;
    2. notifications/initialized;
    3. tools/list;
    4. tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt with a valid prompt_text; and
    5. verification of the returned TextContent, structuredContent, isError state, and a2 stage.
    • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE
      4.2.2. Exact Tool Inventory Acceptance UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      STATEMENT: The tools/list acceptance result shall contain ghost_engineer_prompt and shall contain no additional GHOST tool unless the requirement set has been explicitly extended and the client integration has been rescanned.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE
      4.2.3. PreProcessing-before-A2 Acceptance UID: GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      STATEMENT: An integration test shall prove that the adapter passes the raw prompt through existing PreProcessing before invoking existing A2 on the same SuperPrompt and that the returned prompt_ready value is obtained only after the SuperPrompt stage becomes a2.
    • GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE
      4.2.4. Concurrent Isolation Acceptance UID: GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      STATEMENT: A concurrency test using distinguishable prompts shall prove that simultaneous tool calls return the engineered prompt associated with each respective input and do not exchange or overwrite request-specific SuperPrompt state.
    • GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE
      4.3.1. ChatGPT End-to-End Acceptance UID: GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      STATEMENT: Using the owner's ChatGPT web account with the GHOST custom MCP plugin enabled, an acceptance test shall demonstrate that:

      1. the GHOST server can be registered and authenticated;
      2. ChatGPT can discover ghost_engineer_prompt;
      3. a prompt written by the user can be supplied as prompt_text in the MCP tool call;
      4. GHOST executes PreProcessing and A2; and
      5. the engineered prompt returned by the MCP server is presented in the same ChatGPT conversation.

      A server that passes only local tests or MCP Inspector tests shall not satisfy this requirement.
    • GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE
      4.3.2. Claude End-to-End Acceptance UID: GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      STATEMENT: Using Claude with the GHOST remote custom connector enabled, an acceptance test shall demonstrate that:

      1. the GHOST server can be registered and authenticated;
      2. Claude can discover ghost_engineer_prompt;
      3. a prompt written by the user can be supplied as prompt_text in the MCP tool call;
      4. GHOST executes PreProcessing and A2; and
      5. the engineered prompt returned by the MCP server is presented in the same Claude conversation.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
      4.2.1. MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE Exact Tool Inventory Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE PreProcessing-before-A2 Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE Concurrent Isolation Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE ChatGPT End-to-End Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE Claude End-to-End Acceptance
      STATEMENT: The server shall pass an acceptance sequence that performs, in order:

      1. initialize;
      2. notifications/initialized;
      3. tools/list;
      4. tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt with a valid prompt_text; and
      5. verification of the returned TextContent, structuredContent, isError state, and a2 stage.
    GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE
    4.2.2. Exact Tool Inventory Acceptance UID: GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
    STATEMENT: The tools/list acceptance result shall contain ghost_engineer_prompt and shall contain no additional GHOST tool unless the requirement set has been explicitly extended and the client integration has been rescanned.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
      4.2.1. MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE Exact Tool Inventory Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE PreProcessing-before-A2 Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE Concurrent Isolation Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE ChatGPT End-to-End Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE Claude End-to-End Acceptance
      STATEMENT: The server shall pass an acceptance sequence that performs, in order:

      1. initialize;
      2. notifications/initialized;
      3. tools/list;
      4. tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt with a valid prompt_text; and
      5. verification of the returned TextContent, structuredContent, isError state, and a2 stage.
    GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE
    4.2.3. PreProcessing-before-A2 Acceptance UID: GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
    STATEMENT: An integration test shall prove that the adapter passes the raw prompt through existing PreProcessing before invoking existing A2 on the same SuperPrompt and that the returned prompt_ready value is obtained only after the SuperPrompt stage becomes a2.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
      4.2.1. MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE Exact Tool Inventory Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE PreProcessing-before-A2 Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE Concurrent Isolation Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE ChatGPT End-to-End Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE Claude End-to-End Acceptance
      STATEMENT: The server shall pass an acceptance sequence that performs, in order:

      1. initialize;
      2. notifications/initialized;
      3. tools/list;
      4. tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt with a valid prompt_text; and
      5. verification of the returned TextContent, structuredContent, isError state, and a2 stage.
    GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE
    4.2.4. Concurrent Isolation Acceptance UID: GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
    STATEMENT: A concurrency test using distinguishable prompts shall prove that simultaneous tool calls return the engineered prompt associated with each respective input and do not exchange or overwrite request-specific SuperPrompt state.

    4.3. 4.3. Real-Client Acceptance
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
      4.2.1. MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE Exact Tool Inventory Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE PreProcessing-before-A2 Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE Concurrent Isolation Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE ChatGPT End-to-End Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE Claude End-to-End Acceptance
      STATEMENT: The server shall pass an acceptance sequence that performs, in order:

      1. initialize;
      2. notifications/initialized;
      3. tools/list;
      4. tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt with a valid prompt_text; and
      5. verification of the returned TextContent, structuredContent, isError state, and a2 stage.
    GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE
    4.3.1. ChatGPT End-to-End Acceptance UID: GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
    STATEMENT: Using the owner's ChatGPT web account with the GHOST custom MCP plugin enabled, an acceptance test shall demonstrate that:

    1. the GHOST server can be registered and authenticated;
    2. ChatGPT can discover ghost_engineer_prompt;
    3. a prompt written by the user can be supplied as prompt_text in the MCP tool call;
    4. GHOST executes PreProcessing and A2; and
    5. the engineered prompt returned by the MCP server is presented in the same ChatGPT conversation.

    A server that passes only local tests or MCP Inspector tests shall not satisfy this requirement.
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
      4.2.1. MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence UID: GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-TOOL-INVENTORY-ACCEPTANCE Exact Tool Inventory Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-PROCESSING-ORDER-ACCEPTANCE PreProcessing-before-A2 Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-ISOLATION-ACCEPTANCE Concurrent Isolation Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE ChatGPT End-to-End Acceptance
      • GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE Claude End-to-End Acceptance
      STATEMENT: The server shall pass an acceptance sequence that performs, in order:

      1. initialize;
      2. notifications/initialized;
      3. tools/list;
      4. tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt with a valid prompt_text; and
      5. verification of the returned TextContent, structuredContent, isError state, and a2 stage.
    GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE
    4.3.2. Claude End-to-End Acceptance UID: GHOST-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
    STATEMENT: Using Claude with the GHOST remote custom connector enabled, an acceptance test shall demonstrate that:

    1. the GHOST server can be registered and authenticated;
    2. Claude can discover ghost_engineer_prompt;
    3. a prompt written by the user can be supplied as prompt_text in the MCP tool call;
    4. GHOST executes PreProcessing and A2; and
    5. the engineered prompt returned by the MCP server is presented in the same Claude conversation.
    • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY
      2.1.1. ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility UID: GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-REMOTE-STREAMABLE-HTTP Remote Streamable HTTP Transport
      • GHOST-MCP-EXPLICIT-CLIENT-INVOCATION Explicit Client Invocation Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CROSS-CLIENT-CONSISTENCY Cross-Client Interface Consistency
      STATEMENT: The same GHOST MCP server implementation shall be interoperable with:

      - the owner's ChatGPT web client through its custom MCP plugin capability; and
      - Claude through its remote custom connector capability.

      Compatibility shall be demonstrated against both real clients and shall not be inferred only from an MCP Inspector or a unit-test client.
    GHOST-MCP-CROSS-CLIENT-CONSISTENCY
    4.3.3. Cross-Client Interface Consistency UID: GHOST-MCP-CROSS-CLIENT-CONSISTENCY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
    STATEMENT: ChatGPT and Claude shall use the same MCP endpoint contract, tool identifier, input schema, processing sequence, and output schema.

    Client-specific UI configuration or OAuth registration details shall not create two different GHOST prompt-engineering behaviors.

    5. 5. Ownership Boundaries and Future Extension

    5.1. 5.1. Requirement Ownership Boundaries
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP
      1.3. MCP Adapter Ownership UID: GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE PreProcessing and A2 Functional Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Agent Stack Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Prompt Projection Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE AWS Deployment Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY Native Client and GHOST UI Boundary
      STATEMENT: This document shall own the external MCP protocol contract, the exposed tool contract, request isolation, result delivery, interface-visible failures, and real-client interoperability criteria.

      It shall not own the internal functional semantics of PreProcessing or A2 PromptShaper and shall not duplicate their requirements.
    GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE
    5.1.1. PreProcessing and A2 Functional Ownership UID: GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
    STATEMENT: 30_preprocessing_prompt_functions.sdoc shall remain the owner of deterministic PreProcessing behavior, SuperPrompt preparation behavior, A2 PromptShaper functional behavior, configured meta-field selection, selector validation, and prompt-ready projection outcomes used by this MCP tool.

    This document shall require reuse of those capabilities but shall not restate their internal algorithms.
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP
      1.3. MCP Adapter Ownership UID: GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE PreProcessing and A2 Functional Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Agent Stack Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Prompt Projection Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE AWS Deployment Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY Native Client and GHOST UI Boundary
      STATEMENT: This document shall own the external MCP protocol contract, the exposed tool contract, request isolation, result delivery, interface-visible failures, and real-client interoperability criteria.

      It shall not own the internal functional semantics of PreProcessing or A2 PromptShaper and shall not duplicate their requirements.
    GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE
    5.1.2. Agent Stack Ownership Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
    STATEMENT: 20_agent_stack.sdoc shall remain the owner of generic AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, LLMClient, configuration loading, model-call, parsing, and neutral agent-stack infrastructure used by A2.

    The MCP adapter shall consume that infrastructure only through the existing A2 boundary.
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP
      1.3. MCP Adapter Ownership UID: GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE PreProcessing and A2 Functional Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Agent Stack Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Prompt Projection Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE AWS Deployment Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY Native Client and GHOST UI Boundary
      STATEMENT: This document shall own the external MCP protocol contract, the exposed tool contract, request isolation, result delivery, interface-visible failures, and real-client interoperability criteria.

      It shall not own the internal functional semantics of PreProcessing or A2 PromptShaper and shall not duplicate their requirements.
    GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE
    5.1.3. Prompt Projection Ownership Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
    STATEMENT: The central SuperPrompt projection and prompt-ready composition requirements shall remain owned by the applicable context-integration and SuperPrompt-projection requirement files.

    The MCP adapter shall return the resulting prompt_ready value and shall not become a second prompt renderer.
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP
      1.3. MCP Adapter Ownership UID: GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE PreProcessing and A2 Functional Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Agent Stack Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Prompt Projection Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE AWS Deployment Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY Native Client and GHOST UI Boundary
      STATEMENT: This document shall own the external MCP protocol contract, the exposed tool contract, request isolation, result delivery, interface-visible failures, and real-client interoperability criteria.

      It shall not own the internal functional semantics of PreProcessing or A2 PromptShaper and shall not duplicate their requirements.
    GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE
    5.1.4. AWS Deployment Ownership Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
    STATEMENT: 80_runtime_platform_and_operations.sdoc and 81_aws_deployment_and_operations.sdoc shall own the MCP container deployment, public endpoint, HTTPS termination, reverse proxy, internal port, AWS identity and network controls, secrets delivery, startup, restart, and operational verification.

    This document shall not duplicate concrete AWS account values, ports, paths, container names, ECR repositories, startup scripts, systemd units, nginx blocks, or authorization-provider implementation details.
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP
      1.3. MCP Adapter Ownership UID: GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-PREPROCESSING-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE PreProcessing and A2 Functional Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-AGENT-STACK-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Agent Stack Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-PROJECTION-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE Prompt Projection Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-AWS-OWNERSHIP-REFERENCE AWS Deployment Ownership Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY Native Client and GHOST UI Boundary
      STATEMENT: This document shall own the external MCP protocol contract, the exposed tool contract, request isolation, result delivery, interface-visible failures, and real-client interoperability criteria.

      It shall not own the internal functional semantics of PreProcessing or A2 PromptShaper and shall not duplicate their requirements.
    GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY
    5.1.5. Native Client and GHOST UI Boundary UID: GHOST-MCP-UI-OWNERSHIP-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
    STATEMENT: The MCP server shall not own the native ChatGPT or Claude conversation UI and shall not modify the current Streamlit workbench or the future React/FastAPI interface architecture.

    This document requires the engineered prompt to be delivered to the invoking client; rendering and presentation inside the native client remain client behavior verified through acceptance testing.

    5.2. 5.2. Excluded Capabilities
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT
      1.1. GHOST MCP Interface Scope UID: GHOST-MCP-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Interface Family Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY Product and Python Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR Malformed MCP Request Error
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one remote MCP interface that allows ChatGPT and Claude to submit a user prompt, execute the approved GHOST prompt-engineering path, and receive the resulting engineered prompt in the same client conversation.

      This requirement defines a target interface. The MCP capability shall not be represented as implemented until the server code and the real-client acceptance tests defined by this document have passed.
    GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS
    5.2.1. Excluded GHOST Capabilities UID: GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-NO-PREVIOUS-RESPONSE-CAPTURE No Previous Assistant Response Capture
    • GHOST-MCP-NO-SHARED-BLACKBOARD No Shared Blackboard in Current Contract
    STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt tool shall not execute:

    - ActiveBrief discovery, relation classification, snapshotting, or routing;
    - document ingestion;
    - dense, sparse, hybrid, or reranked document retrieval;
    - evidence selection or condensation;
    - memory recording, ingestion, retrieval, merging, or synthesis;
    - blackboard read or write operations;
    - A3, A4, or later pipeline agents;
    - file management;
    - Streamlit or React/FastAPI UI actions; or
    - final answer generation.
    • GHOST-MCP-NO-PREVIOUS-RESPONSE-CAPTURE
      5.2.2. No Previous Assistant Response Capture UID: GHOST-MCP-NO-PREVIOUS-RESPONSE-CAPTURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
      STATEMENT: The approved MCP interface shall not attempt to obtain, record, or infer a previous ChatGPT or Claude assistant response.

      Conversation-response capture requires a separately approved input contract and shall not be implied by the current prompt_text-only tool.
    • GHOST-MCP-NO-SHARED-BLACKBOARD
      5.2.3. No Shared Blackboard in Current Contract UID: GHOST-MCP-NO-SHARED-BLACKBOARD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
      STATEMENT: The approved MCP interface shall not create or maintain the future shared blackboard between ChatGPT, Claude, and GHOST.

      The future blackboard vision shall not be represented as current behavior merely because the MCP server provides a remote integration point.
    • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS
      5.2.1. Excluded GHOST Capabilities UID: GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-PREVIOUS-RESPONSE-CAPTURE No Previous Assistant Response Capture
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-SHARED-BLACKBOARD No Shared Blackboard in Current Contract
      STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt tool shall not execute:

      - ActiveBrief discovery, relation classification, snapshotting, or routing;
      - document ingestion;
      - dense, sparse, hybrid, or reranked document retrieval;
      - evidence selection or condensation;
      - memory recording, ingestion, retrieval, merging, or synthesis;
      - blackboard read or write operations;
      - A3, A4, or later pipeline agents;
      - file management;
      - Streamlit or React/FastAPI UI actions; or
      - final answer generation.
    GHOST-MCP-NO-PREVIOUS-RESPONSE-CAPTURE
    5.2.2. No Previous Assistant Response Capture UID: GHOST-MCP-NO-PREVIOUS-RESPONSE-CAPTURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
    STATEMENT: The approved MCP interface shall not attempt to obtain, record, or infer a previous ChatGPT or Claude assistant response.

    Conversation-response capture requires a separately approved input contract and shall not be implied by the current prompt_text-only tool.
    • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS
      5.2.1. Excluded GHOST Capabilities UID: GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-PREVIOUS-RESPONSE-CAPTURE No Previous Assistant Response Capture
      • GHOST-MCP-NO-SHARED-BLACKBOARD No Shared Blackboard in Current Contract
      STATEMENT: The ghost_engineer_prompt tool shall not execute:

      - ActiveBrief discovery, relation classification, snapshotting, or routing;
      - document ingestion;
      - dense, sparse, hybrid, or reranked document retrieval;
      - evidence selection or condensation;
      - memory recording, ingestion, retrieval, merging, or synthesis;
      - blackboard read or write operations;
      - A3, A4, or later pipeline agents;
      - file management;
      - Streamlit or React/FastAPI UI actions; or
      - final answer generation.
    GHOST-MCP-NO-SHARED-BLACKBOARD
    5.2.3. No Shared Blackboard in Current Contract UID: GHOST-MCP-NO-SHARED-BLACKBOARD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
    STATEMENT: The approved MCP interface shall not create or maintain the future shared blackboard between ChatGPT, Claude, and GHOST.

    The future blackboard vision shall not be represented as current behavior merely because the MCP server provides a remote integration point.

    5.3. 5.3. Controlled Future Extension
    Future GHOST MCP capabilities may extend this interface to record prior assistant responses, ingest conversation turns into memory, retrieve shared context, and exchange blackboard state across clients. Those capabilities are intentionally not specified as active behavior here.
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT
      1.1. GHOST MCP Interface Scope UID: GHOST-MCP-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Interface Family Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-APPROVED-FLOW Approved Prompt-Engineering Flow
      • GHOST-MCP-INTERFACE-OWNERSHIP MCP Adapter Ownership
      • GHOST-MCP-PRODUCT-PACKAGE-BOUNDARY Product and Python Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-MCP-CLIENT-COMPATIBILITY ChatGPT and Claude Compatibility
      • GHOST-MCP-MALFORMED-REQUEST-ERROR Malformed MCP Request Error
      • GHOST-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP Protocol Acceptance Sequence
      • GHOST-MCP-CURRENT-EXCLUSIONS Excluded GHOST Capabilities
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one remote MCP interface that allows ChatGPT and Claude to submit a user prompt, execute the approved GHOST prompt-engineering path, and receive the resulting engineered prompt in the same client conversation.

      This requirement defines a target interface. The MCP capability shall not be represented as implemented until the server code and the real-client acceptance tests defined by this document have passed.
    GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL
    5.3.1. Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension UID: GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MCP-CONTRACT-CHANGE-CONTROL MCP Tool Contract Change Control
    STATEMENT: A future MCP capability shall be added only through explicit requirements that define its inputs, outputs, persistence effects, authorization needs, ownership boundary, and client acceptance criteria.

    The existence of an extension idea shall not authorize silent expansion of ghost_engineer_prompt.
    • GHOST-MCP-CONTRACT-CHANGE-CONTROL
      5.3.2. MCP Tool Contract Change Control UID: GHOST-MCP-CONTRACT-CHANGE-CONTROL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
      STATEMENT: A change to the ghost_engineer_prompt tool identifier, inputSchema, outputSchema, result semantics, or persistence behavior shall be treated as an interface-contract change.

      Before deployment, the change shall be reflected in approved requirements and shall be followed by refreshed tool discovery and repeated ChatGPT and Claude acceptance testing.
    • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL
      5.3.1. Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension UID: GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-MCP-ROOT GHOST MCP Interface Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-MCP-CONTRACT-CHANGE-CONTROL MCP Tool Contract Change Control
      STATEMENT: A future MCP capability shall be added only through explicit requirements that define its inputs, outputs, persistence effects, authorization needs, ownership boundary, and client acceptance criteria.

      The existence of an extension idea shall not authorize silent expansion of ghost_engineer_prompt.
    GHOST-MCP-CONTRACT-CHANGE-CONTROL
    5.3.2. MCP Tool Contract Change Control UID: GHOST-MCP-CONTRACT-CHANGE-CONTROL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MCP-EXTENSION-APPROVAL Explicit Approval for MCP Scope Extension
    STATEMENT: A change to the ghost_engineer_prompt tool identifier, inputSchema, outputSchema, result semantics, or persistence behavior shall be treated as an interface-contract change.

    Before deployment, the change shall be reflected in approved requirements and shall be followed by refreshed tool discovery and repeated ChatGPT and Claude acceptance testing.
